item.title: Creating Personal GitHub Page
item.description:
This note puts together things I did to create my personal GitHub page using the template `starter-academic' for Hugo. It seems that there may be more efficient ways than what I did.

I first cloned the template to my local computer and added content, I then deployed the web page to GitHub. Everything is done on my laptop with the Fedora 33 Linux system.

1. Preparation

On local computer

I created a folder myAcademicSite to hold all source files. I also installed Go and Hugo-extended.

In terminal:

sudo dnf install golang

Note, I did not install Go by snap as suggested here.

Next, I downloaded the hugo-extended from https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/releases. Uncompress the .gz file, then move the binary hugo to the folder myAcademicSite.

On GitHub

Note, I already have my personal GitHub page, using a default theme of GitHub pages. I backed up the content of this existing page.

Login to my account on GitHub. Fork starter-academic. The fork icon is the top right corner of the page.

In my GitHub account, I renamed the forked starter-academic to myAcademicSite.

2. Deploy the starter-academic template to my GitHub page

Below I mostly referenced https://wowchemy.com/docs/guide/deployment/

Create my web page on local computer

In terminal:

git clone https://github.com/shutaocao/myAcademicSite.git myAcademicSite

cd myAcademicSite

git submodule update --init --recursive

Remove public/ if there is one:

rm -r public/

Still in the folder myAcdemicSite, in the file config/_default/config.yaml, set

baseurl = "https://shutaocao.github.io/"

In terminal:

hugo server

Copy the http address http://localhost:1313/ and paste it in browser. This will start the starter-acadmic raw website on my local computer.

Hit keyboard Ctrl+c to stop hugo server.

Deploy the newly created web page to GitHub

In terminal, if not in the folder myAcadmicSite:

cd myAcademicSite

In terminal:

git submodule add -f -b master https://github.com/shutaocao/shutaocao.github.io.git public
git add .
git commit -m "Initial commit"
git push -u origin master

This has done two things:

  • It created a folder publich/ that has all necessary files and folders for my personal GitHub page.

  • It deployed the raw starter-academic template in public/ to GitHub.

Next, regenerate my website’s HTML code by running hugo and pushing the public submodule to GitHub, in terminal:

hugo
cd public
git add .
git commit -m "Build website"
git push origin master
cd ..

This now has deployed the raw starter-academic content as my website to GitHub.

In browser, go to shutaocao.github.io, it looks like this

Now, I have successfully cloned the raw starter-academic page to be my personal GitHub page.

Next, I need to customize my personal GitHub page.

3. Customize My Personal GitHub Page

All changes to my personal GitHub page are done on my local computer. I then push them to GitHub by repeating the following:

hugo
cd public
git add .
git commit -m "Build website"
git push origin master
cd ..

It is a little tedious for me as a newbie to customize my personal web page. The first thing should have been to understand the template structure in the folder myAcademicSite, instead I did other things which is not efficient.

In customizing the content of my personal website, I referenced wowchemy website.

https://wowchemy.com/docs/getting-started/get-started/

4. A Thank You Note

I thank George Cushen for making the template open source, I consider to show my support and appreciation to his work.

The post by Mickaël Lalande appears more complete, thank you too.

https://mickaellalande.github.io/post/tutorial/how-to-create-an-academic-github-page-with-hugo/
